label: Probabilistic & differentiable programming

node.label: Probabilistic & differentiable programming
node.definition: Probabilistic programming expresses generative assumptions as executable programs and delegates posterior inference to sampling, variational, or differentiable machinery.
node.why_it_matters: The role of Probabilistic & differentiable programming is to connect Programs, ASTs, bytecode & intermediate representations, Bayesian & approximate inference to downstream capabilities including Scientific ML, simulators & digital twins, Knowledge graphs, semantic search & relational reasoning.
examples: substrate for Scientific ML, simulators & digital twins
substrate for Knowledge graphs, semantic search & relational reasoning
limitations: Its inductive bias, data demand, optimization behavior, and compute profile can make it unsuitable outside the regimes where it was validated. Its direct dependencies include Programs, ASTs, bytecode & intermediate representations, Bayesian & approximate inference.

dependencies.rationale: Probabilistic & differentiable programming consumes, emits, or organizes information using Programs, ASTs, bytecode & intermediate representations.
Probabilistic & differentiable programming is learned, selected, or evaluated using the objective family Bayesian & approximate inference.
Probabilistic & differentiable programming is assembled in part from the mechanism or model component Reparameterization & differentiable sampling.
Probabilistic & differentiable programming is assembled in part from the mechanism or model component Constraint propagation, logic & program execution.
